A degree in mathematics provides entry to many careers in industry in addition to teaching. For instance, operations research, data analysis, systems analysis, computing, actuarial work, insurance, and financial services are only a few such careers. Mathematics is also a sound basis for graduate work in a variety of fields, such as law, engineering, and economics.

Group 1: Alternative combinations may be acceptable. Please refer to the major and an advisor.

Group 2 and Group 3: Please see the Mathematics department agreements for a possible applicable MAT 022AB series articulation agreement.

Group 4: Basic knowledge of MATLAB is required for both MAT 022A and MAT 067. Students can learn it on their own, enroll in ENG 006 or in the 1 unit course MAT 022AL (can be taken concurrently)

Mathematics is the study of abstract structures, space, change, and the interrelations of these concepts. It also is the language of the exact sciences.


After completing basic introductory courses such as calculus and linear algebra, students plan an upper division program in consultation with a faculty advisor. Upper division courses include real analysis, probability, modern algebra, as well as a variety of other courses that allow students to further mathematical knowledge and skills that feature their research or career interests. This individualized program can lead to graduate study in pure or applied mathematics, elementary or secondary level teaching, or to other professional goals. It can also reflect a special interest such as computational and applied mathematics, computer science, or statistics, or may be combined with a major in some other field.

","groupId":"10b46f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"THE MAJOR PROGRAM","groupId":"11b46f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"PREPARATION COURSES FOR THE MAJOR","groupId":"3a127945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below and maintain a 3.00 GPA across the entire group:

●    Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D

●    Mathematics (MAT) 022A or 027A or 067

●    Mathematics (MAT) 022B or 027B


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

","groupId":"a8b56f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

At the time of TAG submission, Math and Scientific Computation requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.00 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
